meta.title: Maiga • Malian Student in Japan
hero.desc: Malian student in Japan, passionate about tech, AI, and building projects that help foreigners living in Japan. Future cybersecurity engineer.
about.label: About
about.sub: From Mali to study cybersecurity in Japan, I build platforms that make life easier for foreigners. I share my journey with honesty.
about.t1.p: First passion for tech, self-taught web development.
about.t2.p: Culture shock, unknown language, new life. The start of a big adventure.
about.t3.p: Intensive training in cybersecurity, networks, Linux, and AWS cloud.
about.t4.p: Launching platforms, creating content, building my online presence.
proj.label: Projects
proj.title: What I build
proj.sub: Real projects, real tech, for real needs.
p1.title: Foreigners in Japan Platform
p1.desc: A central hub to help foreigners navigate life in Japan: housing, paperwork, work, language.
p2.title: School Management System
p2.desc: Full app to manage students, grades, timetables, and communications for schools.
p3.title: Interactive Blog
p3.desc: Blog platform with comments, user profiles, and likes. Built-in community.
p4.title: Student App
p4.desc: Tool for international students in Japan: budget, classes, reminders, calendar, and useful resources.
p5.title: AI Projects
p5.desc: Experiments with local AI models, home AI server, and smart automations.
p6.title: REST API System
p6.desc: Modular PHP and MySQL API to power several projects from one unified backend.
blog.label: Blog
blog.sub: Honest articles about the real experience of an African student in Japan.
b1.title: Arriving in Japan: between dream and reality
b1.excerpt: What nobody told you before you came. The first month, culture shock, language, loneliness, and first wins. My full story...
b2.title: What student life in Japan really costs
b2.excerpt: Rent, food, transport, leisure. Real numbers from a student.
b3.title: How to find a baito (part-time job) in Japan
b3.excerpt: Practical tips, sites to know, and mistakes to avoid.
b4.title: How I improve my Japanese every day
b4.excerpt: Concrete methods, apps, immersion tips outside school.
b5.title: Osaka vs Tokyo: a student's take
b5.excerpt: Culture, prices, vibe, people. My honest comparison.
b6.title: Mistakes I made when I arrived
b6.excerpt: So you don't repeat them. Brutally honest.
japan.label: Life in Japan
japan.sub: Everything the tourist guides don't spell out.
j1.p: Procedures, renewals, Japanese bureaucracy explained simply.
j2.p: Finding a flat without a Japanese guarantor, dorms, real costs.
j3.p: Living on a tight budget: supermarkets, apps, practical tips.
j4.p: IC cards, trains, buses, bikes. Getting around efficiently and cheaply.
j5.p: Resources, methods, level needed for daily situations.
j6.p: Student insurance, finding a doctor, emergencies in Japan.
j7.p: Best SIM deals, pocket WiFi, student-friendly plans.
j8.p: Social codes, common culture shocks, tips to fit in.
